blockingIssues.description: scrapers/property.py uses normalized county in cache key but raw county for routing; inputs like ' BROWARD ' can route to Miami-Dade and poison the ('folio','broward') cache entry with wrong data.
pipeline/leads.py constructs ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=len(enabled_counties)); when zero counties are enabled this raises ValueError instead of gracefully proceeding.
tests/test_concurrent_caching.py concurrency assertion can pass for sequential execution, so it does not actually validate parallel county scraping per contract intent.
scrapers/retry_utils.py retries broad requests.RequestException, which includes non-transient failures; contract requires retry only on transient statuses plus ConnectionError/Timeout class failures.
src/App.tsx URL-state sync skips empty query strings; navigating back to '/' can leave stale filter state instead of restoring defaults, breaking history consistency.
tests/test_cross_area_integration.py does not validate VAL-CROSS-006 through dashboard rendering/browser behavior; it only exercises backend helpers.
Graceful-degradation tests catch voter exceptions directly in test code instead of asserting continuation through the actual build_canonical_lead_dataset orchestration path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit RED→GREEN evidence in transcripts (first failing test run before implementation edits), and require contract-faithful assertions when contracts specify measurable thresholds.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for concurrent-scrapes-and-caching, scraper-retry-logic, and db-schema-migration all noted implementation edits before tests; concurrent-scrapes test used a proxy assertion that can pass sequentially despite contract-level concurrency expectations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a feature expectedBehavior includes browser history semantics (back/forward), add matching validation-contract assertions so those behaviors are mandatory in validation, not implicit.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: url-state-persistence feature expected back/forward preservation, but validation-contract only covered URL reflection and preload (VAL-INFRA-011/012), and a back-navigation stale-state bug escaped feature verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require explicit assertion-to-feature ownership consistency checks when tool modality differs (e.g., agent-browser assertion assigned to python-only feature).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: VAL-CROSS-006 is defined as agent-browser in validation-contract but assigned to cross-area-integration-tests (python-backend) in features.json, creating ambiguous implementation scope and missed UI-layer verification.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an auditable console-check step (e.g., required agent-browser console command + transcript evidence) and reinforce using .factory/services.yaml start/stop commands during validation runs.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: mobile-responsive-layout review found no explicit console-read evidence and observed ad-hoc dev-server lifecycle commands instead of declared service commands.

rejectedObservations.observation: Undocumented websockets.asyncio import caveat for db.insert test imports.
Verification-only feature handoffs referencing unrelated commit IDs should be standardized immediately.

orchestratorOverrides.lintFailure: Known pre-existing repository issue documented in mission AGENTS.md (eslint binary/config absent). Treated as non-blocking validator noise for scrutiny synthesis.
